summary: Completed Patch 12m phantom-drop guard testing. Backend 12g bail-out path is triggering correctly (SegFormer mask < 40%), but the 12m phantom-drop guard (alpha coverage < 8%) is NOT firing as expected. However, NO white-window artifacts were observed in the frontend closet, suggesting rembg is producing adequate alpha coverage even when SegFormer fails. The workStore 404 handling is working correctly.

observations.issue: The 12m phantom-drop guard is not firing even though 12g bail-out path is triggered
observations.evidence: Backend logs show 'SegFormer mask too patchy (5.2%, 27.7%, 19.6%, 14.9% coverage < 40% threshold)' messages, but NO 'Background matte DROPPING phantom item' messages
observations.impact: LOW - Despite guard not firing, no white-window artifacts observed in closet
observations.hypothesis: rembg is producing alpha coverage >= 8% even when SegFormer fails, so the guard condition (both detectors disagree AND alpha < 8%) is not met
observations.fix_priority: MEDIUM - Investigate why guard isn't firing, but not critical since no white windows observed

working_correctly.evidence: Multiple log entries showing 'SegFormer mask too patchy' with coverage percentages below 40%
All items completed polish with status 'ready', no failures observed
47 items in closet, all displaying correctly with proper thumbnails
Frontend test found 96 visible items, 0 images with low opacity (< 0.3)
Polish poll completed successfully for all items, no stuck 'Polishing N/M' counters

passed_tests: WorkStore poll handled all items without getting stuck
Frontend closet displays all items correctly
No white-window artifacts detected in closet
Regression test: normal garments (tops/bottoms) saved successfully

success_percentage.backend: 75% - 12g bail-out working, but 12m guard not firing as expected
success_percentage.frontend: 100% - All features working correctly, no white windows observed

items.description: The 12m phantom-drop guard in _run_background_matte() is not firing even though the 12g bail-out path is triggered. Backend logs show 'SegFormer mask too patchy' messages but NO 'DROPPING phantom item' messages.
items.investigation_needed: Check if rembg is consistently producing alpha coverage >= 8% even when SegFormer fails. If so, the guard is working as designed (only drops when BOTH detectors fail). If rembg is producing good mattes, the guard may not be needed.

items.description: Add logging to _rembg_alpha_coverage_pct() to see actual alpha coverage percentages when 12g bail-out happens
items.suggested_fix: Add debug logging: logger.info(f'Alpha coverage check: {alpha_pct*100:.1f}% (threshold {_PHANTOM_DROP_ALPHA_THRESHOLD*100:.1f}%)')

positive_findings: NO white-window artifacts observed in production closet despite guard not firing
This suggests rembg is producing adequate mattes even when SegFormer fails
Frontend polish poll is not getting stuck

additional_notes.conclusion: The phantom-drop guard is not firing, but this may be by design if rembg is producing adequate alpha coverage. The absence of white-window artifacts in production suggests the system is working correctly overall. Recommend adding debug logging to verify alpha coverage percentages and confirm guard is functioning as intended.
